  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head, Banking Operations",
  "description": "AAA Finance and Investment Company Limited is a fast growing Finance House company established in 2018 and promoted by reputable investors. AA Finance and Investment Company limited is registered and licensed by Central Bank of Nigeria as a Finance House &nbsp;to provide financial services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Banking Operations

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Requirements


	Strong understanding of financial operations, treasury, accounting including FX, cashflow, financial analysis and reporting and various financial instruments
	Degree from an accredited institution or any other professional certification with 2-3 year proven work experience in a financial institution in a managerial role
	Proficiency in using financial software, CRM systems and Enterprise
	Resource Planning (ERP) Tools
	Experience with regulatory, compliance and financial industry standards.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Application to:&nbsp;hiring@aaafinancegroup.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Head, Banking Operations Job at AAA Finance and Investment Company Limited




        
    

        
        AAA Finance and Investment Company Limited is a fast growing Finance House company established in 2018 and promoted by reputable investors. AA Finance and Investment Company limited is registered and licensed by Central Bank of Nigeria as a Finance House  to provide financial services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Banking Operations

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Strong understanding of financial operations, treasury, accounting including FX, cashflow, financial analysis and reporting and various financial instruments
	Degree from an accredited institution or any other professional certification with 2-3 year proven work experience in a financial institution in a managerial role
	Proficiency in using financial software, CRM systems and Enterprise
	Resource Planning (ERP) Tools
	Experience with regulatory, compliance and financial industry standards.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Auditor",
  "description": "Westfield Consulting Limited -&nbsp;Our client, with interest in Real Estate, Energy, Agriculture, Minning, Maritime and Aviation, is recruiting qualified and passionate candidate&nbsp;to fill the position below:

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Responsible forproviding assurance on the adequacy, appropriateness and effectiveness of the organizations internal control environment and risk management framework


Key Responsibilities
Audit Planning and Execution:


	Support in the developing and implementing annual audit plans based on risk assessments, industry standards, and regulatory requirements.
	Conduct financial, operational, and compliance audits to assess the effectiveness of internal controls and adherence to company policies and procedures.
	Ensure audits are conducted in accordance with audit methodology and in line with local and international standards
	Perform risk-based audits, ensuring a focus on key areas such as revenue leakages, inventory management, procurement, and regulatory compliance.


Risk Assessment and Internal Control Evaluation:


	Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls and processes, identifying weaknesses and potential risks.
	Provide recommendations to mitigate risks, improve operational efficiency, and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements (e.g., NPA, NUPRC, NMDPRA, NIMASA etc).
	Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) related to risk management and internal controls.


Reporting and Communication:


	Prepare detailed audit reports highlighting findings, risks, and recommendations for management and stakeholders.
	Communicate audit findings and provide actionable recommendations to management in a clear and concise manner.
	Follow up on management&rsquo;s corrective actions to ensure implementation of audit recommendations.


Compliance and Regulatory Audits:


	Ensure the company adheres to Nigerian laws and regulations specific to the oil and gas sector.
	Conduct compliance audits related to the Petroleum Industry Act (PIA) and other industry-specific regulations.
	Coordinate with regulatory authorities during audits and ensure that all statutory requirements are met.


Fraud Detection and Prevention:


	Identify and investigate instances of fraud, corruption, and financial mismanagement.
	Develop internal controls to prevent fraud and ensure transparency in financial transactions and reporting.
	Provide guidance to management on fraud risk management and mitigation strategies.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree / HND in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	3-5 years of audit experience, preferably within the oil and gas industry.
	Professional certifications such as ACA, ACCA is mandatory, while CIA (Certified Internal Auditor), or CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor) are highly preferred.
	Strong understanding of the Nigerian oil and gas sector, including regulatory frameworks and key business processes.
	Experience in financial reporting, risk management, and compliance audits.


Salary:&nbsp;Very attractive and Negotiable.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their curent CV to:&nbsp;victor.allu@westfield-consulting.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note:Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-31",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Westfield Consulting Limited"

Internal Auditor Job at Westfield Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Westfield Consulting Limited - Our client, with interest in Real Estate, Energy, Agriculture, Minning, Maritime and Aviation, is recruiting qualified and passionate candidate to fill the position below:

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Responsible forproviding assurance on the adequacy, appropriateness and effectiveness of the organizations internal control environment and risk management framework


Key Responsibilities
Audit Planning and Execution:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Support in the developing and implementing annual audit plans based on risk assessments, industry standards, and regulatory requirements.
	Conduct financial, operational, and compliance audits to assess the effectiveness of internal controls and adherence to company policies and procedures.
	Ensure audits are conducted in accordance with audit methodology and in line with local and international standards
	Perform risk-based audits, ensuring a focus on key areas such as revenue leakages, inventory management, procurement, and regulatory compliance.


Risk Assessment and Internal Control Evaluation:


	Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls and processes, identifying weaknesses and potential risks.
	Provide recommendations to mitigate risks, improve operational efficiency, and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements (e.g., NPA, NUPRC, NMDPRA, NIMASA etc).
	Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) related to risk management and internal controls.


Reporting and Communication:


	Prepare detailed audit reports highlighting findings, risks, and recommendations for management and stakeholders.
	Communicate audit findings and provide actionable recommendations to management in a clear and concise manner.
	Follow up on management’s corrective actions to ensure implementation of audit recommendations.


Compliance and Regulatory Audits:


	Ensure the company adheres to Nigerian laws and regulations specific to the oil and gas sector.
	Conduct compliance audits related to the Petroleum Industry Act (PIA) and other industry-specific regulations.
	Coordinate with regulatory authorities during audits and ensure that all statutory requirements are met.


Fraud Detection and Prevention:


	Identify and investigate instances of fraud, corruption, and financial mismanagement.
	Develop internal controls to prevent fraud and ensure transparency in financial transactions and reporting.
	Provide guidance to management on fraud risk management and mitigation strategies.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree / HND in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	3-5 years of audit experience, preferably within the oil and gas industry.
	Professional certifications such as ACA, ACCA is mandatory, while CIA (Certified Internal Auditor), or CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor) are highly preferred.
	Strong understanding of the Nigerian oil and gas sector, including regulatory frameworks and key business processes.
	Experience in financial reporting, risk management, and compliance audits.


Salary: Very attractive and Negotiable.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their curent CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note:Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Officer",
  "description": "RoadLamp Limited is an EPCI that has operated in the oil and gas sector for over 7 years. We have risen through the ranks of the industry with a growing capacity to dominate our niche EPCI sect. Our increased capacity, experience, field assets, workforce (team composition of experts), excellent knowledge of our business terrain and in-depth understanding of the inner workings of the domestic oil and gas market are the key differentiating factors for our business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Finance Officer will assist in managing all financial activities, including budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, and compliance
	This role requires a deep understanding of financial principles, strong leadership skills, and the ability to drive financial strategies that align with the company&rsquo;s goals.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Planning and Analysis:


	Assist in developing and overseeing financial strategies, budgets, and forecasts.
	Analyze financial performance and provide insights to support strategic decision-making.
	Prepare and present detailed financial reports to senior management and stakeholders.


Budget Management:


	Manage the preparation and implementation of budgets.
	Monitor and report on budget variances, providing recommendations for corrective actions.
	Ensure efficient allocation and utilization of financial resources.


Financial Reporting:


	Preparation of financial statements, ensuring accuracy and compliance with regulatory requirements.
	Ensure timely and accurate financial reporting to stakeholders, including board members and investors.
	Assist in developing and maintaining financial reporting systems and processes.


Cash Flow Management and Treasury:


	Monitor cash flow, liquidity, and working capital requirements to optimize cash management, liquidity management, and treasury operations.
	Receivables &amp; payables management and ensuring timely invoicing and collections.


Compliance and Risk Management:


	Ensure compliance with financial regulations, laws, and company policies.
	Assist in developing and implementing internal controls to safeguard company assets.
	Identify and mitigate financial risks by conducting regular risk assessments.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in financial management, with a track record of progressively responsible roles.
	ACA/CFA certification is compulsory
	Adequate knowledge of QuickBooks or similar software
	Extensive experience in budgeting, financial analysis, and financial reporting.


Skills and Ability:


	Strong knowledge of financial regulations and compliance standards.
	Excellent analytical and strategic thinking skills.
	Proficient in financial management software and advanced Excel skills.
	Exceptional leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities with little or no supervision.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@roadlamp.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Finance Officer Job at RoadLamp Limited




        
    

        
        RoadLamp Limited is an EPCI that has operated in the oil and gas sector for over 7 years. We have risen through the ranks of the industry with a growing capacity to dominate our niche EPCI sect. Our increased capacity, experience, field assets, workforce (team composition of experts), excellent knowledge of our business terrain and in-depth understanding of the inner workings of the domestic oil and gas market are the key differentiating factors for our business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Finance Officer will assist in managing all financial activities, including budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, and compliance
	This role requires a deep understanding of financial principles, strong leadership skills, and the ability to drive financial strategies that align with the company’s goals.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Planning and Analysis:


	Assist in developing and overseeing financial strategies, budgets, and forecasts.
	Analyze financial performance and provide insights to support strategic decision-making.
	Prepare and present detailed financial reports to senior management and stakeholders.


Budget Management:


	Manage the preparation and implementation of budgets.
	Monitor and report on budget variances, providing recommendations for corrective actions.
	Ensure efficient allocation and utilization of financial resources.


Financial Reporting:


	Preparation of financial statements, ensuring accuracy and compliance with regulatory requirements.
	Ensure timely and accurate financial reporting to stakeholders, including board members and investors.
	Assist in developing and maintaining financial reporting systems and processes.


Cash Flow Management and Treasury:


	Monitor cash flow, liquidity, and working capital requirements to optimize cash management, liquidity management, and treasury operations.
	Receivables & payables management and ensuring timely invoicing and collections.


Compliance and Risk Management:


	Ensure compliance with financial regulations, laws, and company policies.
	Assist in developing and implementing internal controls to safeguard company assets.
	Identify and mitigate financial risks by conducting regular risk assessments.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in financial management, with a track record of progressively responsible roles.
	ACA/CFA certification is compulsory
	Adequate knowledge of QuickBooks or similar software
	Extensive experience in budgeting, financial analysis, and financial reporting.


Skills and Ability:


	Strong knowledge of financial regulations and compliance standards.
	Excellent analytical and strategic thinking skills.
	Proficient in financial management software and advanced Excel skills.
	Exceptional leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities with little or no supervision.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application

Employment Type: Full-time

	Make outbound calls to potential customers.
	Generate leads and close sales.
	Promote products or services.
	Build customer relationships.
	Build relationships with existing customers to drive repeat business.
